hypothesis: When energy consumption (computed from reactant/product heat capacities and reaction enthalpies) and estimated purification costs are incorporated into the economic-efficiency metric, the higher-thermodynamic-yield Ca3SiO5+Si3N4 route will become more cost-effective than CaO+SiO2 for Ca2SiO4 production at scales above 100 kg and when energy prices exceed 0.10 €/kWh.
